title: Conjecture 1.2
statement_text: Let $\mathcal{C}$ be a class of connected graphs, closed under contracting edges and subdividing edges. For all $L,C$ there exists $C^{\prime}$ such that if there is an $(L,C)$-quasi-isometry from a graph $G$ to a member of $\mathcal{C}$, then there is a $(1,C^{\prime})$-quasi-isometry from $G$ to a member of $\mathcal{C}$.
context_text: Motivated by a theorem of Chepoi, Dragan, Newman, Rabinovich, and Vaxès showing that $(L,C)$-quasi-isometry to a tree implies $(1,C')$-quasi-isometry to a tree, the authors ask for which graph classes $\mathcal{C}$ such a multiplicative-to-additive distortion improvement holds. Closure under edge subdivision and edge contraction is identified as a necessary condition, and the conjecture proposes it is also sufficient. The paper proves a special case: when $\mathcal{C}$ is the class of graphs of bounded path-width.
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. II. Path-width and additive quasi-isometry
